data.title: ATEX Resources: Buy Rating Backed by Valeriano Resource Growth, Nuevo Horizonte Upside, and Strategic Financial Positioning
data.description:
Analyst Derick Ma of TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on ATEX Resources Inc, with a price target of C$4.75.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Derick Ma has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including strong indications that ATEX can grow its high-grade, near-surface resource at the Valeriano project. Ongoing and upcoming drill results, particularly from holes ATXD19A and ATXD23C, could extend the B2B zone toward surface and materially enhance project economics by enabling a phased, lower-capex development path.

He also sees significant upside from the large-scale exploration potential at Nuevo Horizonte, where work is expected to advance toward drilling around Q4 2027, leveraging the team’s experience at Valeriano. In addition, the company’s solid financial position, with enough cash to fund at least two more drill seasons, and the presence of strategic shareholders like Agnico Eagle and Pierre Lassonde, reinforce his confidence in both execution capability and future strategic or M&A optionality.

In another report released yesterday, Stifel Nicolaus also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a C$5.25 price target.

ATX’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from C$2.710 to C$3.580, which is a 32.10% increase.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: AB Dynamics: Neutral Stance Maintained Amid Steady Profitability and Sector Headwinds
data.description:
Analyst Vanessa Jeffriess from Jefferies reiterated a Hold rating on AB Dynamics and keeping the price target at p1,370.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Vanessa Jeffriess has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors including a largely uneventful interim update, with results matching prior guidance and offering limited fresh catalysts. Revenue declined year on year and order growth was mixed, yet profitability held steady and management confirmed its full-year outlook, indicating resilience despite uneven demand trends.

At the same time, sector headwinds such as geopolitical tension and automotive industry uncertainty are weighing on near-term growth expectations, even though long-term structural demand and a strong balance sheet provide strategic flexibility, including scope for acquisitions. With forecasts unchanged and the risk-reward profile appearing balanced rather than compelling, Jeffriess concludes that maintaining a neutral stance on AB Dynamics is appropriate at this stage.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
